questions.text: For an adult patient, a fall from which height is considered significant:
questions.feedback: Correct answer: 10 feet. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: For an adult patient, a fall from which height is considered significant. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: The type of surface struck. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: A patient has been critically hurt in a nighttime motor vehicle collision. She was wearing a seat belt and hit a tree at 70 mph. questions.text: You are transporting a stable patient who was involved in a minor fall from a ladder at a height of about 10 feet to a local community hospital. You provide appropirate spinal motion restriction, not only because the patient has midline back pain, but also because he was knocked unconscious. While transporting, the patient begins to become increasingly confused, develop an irregular respiratory rate, and experience a drop in heart rate with an increase in blood pressure. You just called in a radio report and are about 7 minutes from the hospital. A trauma center is about 10 minutes away. Which of the following is your transport decision?

options.label: A
options.text: Divert to the trauma center because the patient is becoming symptomatic.

options.label: B
options.text: Continue transporting to the local hospital because it's the closest facility.

options.label: C
options.text: Continue transporting to the local hospital because you've already given your report and they accepted the patient.

options.label: D
options.text: Call medical control for advice from the trauma center.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Divert to the trauma center because the patient is becoming symptomatic.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: You are transporting a stable patient who was involved in a minor fall from a ladder at a height of about 10 feet to a local community hospital. You provide appropirate spinal motion restriction, not only because the patient has midline back pain, but also because he was knocked unconscious. While transporting, the patient begins to become increasingly confused, develop an irregular respiratory rate, and experience a drop in heart rate with an increase in blood pressure. You just called in a radio report and are about 7 minutes from the hospital. A trauma center is about 10 minutes away. Which of the following is your transport decision. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: A 9-year-old female was struck by a car while she was riding her bicycle. She complains of pain in her left forearm, right shoulder, and abdomen. Her skin is pale and cool. Her right shoulder appears uninjured. You observe an angulated injury to her forearm, and distal pulses are present. She has an abrasion to her right upper abdominal quadrant, and the pain increases with palpation. You should perform spinal motion restriction,

options.label: B
options.text: splint her left arm in the position found, and apply a cold pack.

options.label: C
options.text: splint her left arm, and apply a sling to her right arm.

options.label: D
options.text: gently realign her left arm into the position of function, and apply a splint.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: administer oxygen, and transport immediately.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: A 9-year-old female was struck by a car while she was riding her bicycle. She complains of pain in her left forearm, right shoulder, and abdomen. Her skin is pale and cool. Her right shoulder appears uninjured. questions.text: You're an off-duty EMT who encounters a patient sitting behind the wheel of a vehicle that ran off an isolated county road. questions.text: Your patient is an 11-year-old male who has a swollen, painful, and angulated right lower arm after falling from his bicycle onto his hands. Which of the following should be considered in the immobilization of his injured extremity?

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Check pulse, movement, and sensation distal to the injury before and after splinting.. questions.text: You respond to a 32-year-old male who fell 20 feet off a ladder. He is responsive to painful stimuli. He has snoring respirations at 20 a minute with decent chest rise and fall. questions.text: A young man was riding a motorcycle when he was hit head on by a car and thrown from the bike. Your scene size-up reveals him to be lying supine in the roadway with obvious deformity to his right thigh and left ankle, with blood noted on his pants in these areas. The patient assessment reveals he is unresponsive with snoring respirations and is breathing irregularly at 16 times per minute. His radial pulse is moderate in strength, and his skin is warm and dry. Which action should be performed first by the EMT?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide manual cervical spine motion restriction

options.label: B
options.text: Open the airway using the head-tilt, chin-lift maneuver

options.label: C
options.text: Check for distal pulses in both lower extremities

options.label: D
options.text: Expose and splint both legs to prevent further injury

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Provide manual cervical spine motion restriction. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: A young man was riding a motorcycle when he was hit head on by a car and thrown from the bike. questions.text: Your patient is a 33-year-old man who has a gunshot wound to his right leg and has active, steady, dark red bleeding. He is awake, pale, and diaphoretic. options.label: B

options.label: C

options.label: D

questions.text: Your patient is a 12-year-old boy who ran his arm through a glass window and has an 8-inch laceration on his anterior forearm. You have applied a pressure dressing and bandage, but these have become saturated due to continued bleeding. Which of the following should you do now?

options.label: A
options.text: Elevate the arm and prepare to apply a tourniquet or consider administering a hemostatic agent.

options.label: B
options.text: Remove the pressure dressing and bandage, apply an ice pack to the wound, and bandage it in place with an elastic bandage.

options.label: C
options.text: Apply additional dressing material, bandage it in place, and apply pressure to the brachial artery.

options.label: D
options.text: Remove the pressure dressing and bandage, apply direct pressure with your gloved hand, and elevate the arm.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Elevate the arm and prepare to apply a tourniquet or consider administering a hemostatic agent.. questions.text: You are participating in a training exercise for an attack with a weapon of mass destruction involving the release of a nerve agent. In the triage area, you are presented with a patient who responds to painful stimuli and has an open airway. He is breathing 8 times per minute and has a weak radial pulse. He is wheezing and has pinpoint pupils. In his pocket, you see a metered-dose inhaler containing albuterol. When caring for this patient, you should first:

options.label: B
options.text: Administer albuterol with the metered-dose inhaler

options.label: C
options.text: Look for an injury causing shock

options.label: D
options.text: Obtain vital signs, including the patient's temperature

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Administer positive pressure ventilation. questions.text: You have responded to a bar for a fight involving approximately 8 to 10 patrons. Weapons were used in the fight, and all the patrons have varying degrees and types of injuries. You are in charge of triage and must use the Respirations, Perfusion, Mental Status (RPM) sequence of the Simple Triage and Rapid Transport (START) system. You find a young person lying on the floor with blood covering his shirt. Quick assessment reveals him to be breathing at 24 times per minute. What should you do next?

options.label: C
options.text: Place a yellow tag on his wrist

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Check for a radial pulse. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: You have responded to a bar for a fight involving approximately 8 to 10 patrons. Weapons were used in the fight, and all the patrons have varying degrees and types of injuries. You are in charge of triage and must use the Respirations, Perfusion, Mental Status (RPM) sequence of the Simple Triage and Rapid Transport (START) system. You find a young person lying on the floor with blood covering his shirt. Quick assessment reveals him to be breathing at 24 times per minute. What should you do next. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: You responded to an explosion at an outside concert venue and were assigned to triage. While triaging patients, you should:

options.label: B
options.text: apply pressure dressings to uncontrolled bleeding from an arm injury.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: look for secondary devices.. questions.text: Simple Triage and Rapid Treatment (START) is a system that relies on some simple commands and what three physiologic parameters?
questions.feedback: Correct answer: Respiration, perfusion, and mental status. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: Simple Triage and Rapid Treatment (START) is a system that relies on some simple commands and what three physiologic parameters. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.

questions.text: You are beginning triage at the scene of a mass-casualty incident in which a commuter train has derailed. Which of the following should you do first?

options.label: A
options.text: Announce that everyone who can get up and walk needs to go to the parking lot of a nearby building.

options.label: C
options.text: Do a quick pulse check on all patients at the scene.

options.label: D
options.text: Check the airway status of all patients who do not appear to be moving.

questions.feedback: Correct answer: Announce that everyone who can get up and walk needs to go to the parking lot of a nearby building.. This item is tied to the concept in the question stem: You are beginning triage at the scene of a mass-casualty incident in which a commuter train has derailed. Which of the following should you do first. Review why the keyed option best fits the presentation, protocol, or EMT decision point described.
